title: Phase 3: create ferrotorch-ml workspace crate bridging Tensor <-> ferrolearn (sklearn metrics, preprocessing, CV, decomposition)
description:
Create a new workspace crate ferrotorch-ml that bridges ferrotorch::Tensor to ferrolearn estimators. Ferrolearn uses ndarray::Array2 (not ferray::Array), so a thin adapter layer is required — see docs/audit/16-ferray-ferrolearn-integration.md.

Scope (Phase 3 of the integration audit):
1. Create ferrotorch-ml/ directory with Cargo.toml + lib.rs. Add to workspace members.
2. Add workspace deps:
   - ferrotorch-core (Tensor type)
   - ndarray (already a transitive dep)
   - ferrolearn-metrics
   - ferrolearn-preprocess
   - ferrolearn-model-sel
   - ferrolearn-decomp
   - ferrolearn-datasets
   (NOT ferrolearn-linear/tree/neighbors/cluster/bayes/kernel/neural — those are sklearn ML, parallel to ferrotorch's deep learning. If users want them, expose only a tightly-curated subset later.)
3. Implement Tensor <-> ndarray::Array2 adapters in ferrotorch-ml/src/adapter.rs:
   - tensor_to_ndarray2(t: &Tensor<T>) -> Result<Array2<T>>
   - ndarray2_to_tensor(arr: Array2<T>) -> Tensor<T>
   - GPU tensors must be moved to CPU first; do NOT do this implicitly. Function should take Tensor on Cpu device and return error otherwise. The user MUST call t.to(Device::Cpu) explicitly.
4. Re-export curated surface:
   - ferrotorch_ml::metrics::{accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, roc_auc_score, confusion_matrix, r2_score, mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error, log_loss, brier_score_loss, silhouette_score, ...}
   - ferrotorch_ml::preprocess::{StandardScaler, MinMaxScaler, MaxAbsScaler, RobustScaler, Normalizer, KBinsDiscretizer, OneHotEncoder, OrdinalEncoder, LabelEncoder, PolynomialFeatures}
   - ferrotorch_ml::model_selection::{KFold, StratifiedKFold, GroupKFold, ShuffleSplit, train_test_split, cross_val_score, cross_validate, GridSearchCV, RandomizedSearchCV}
   - ferrotorch_ml::decomposition::{PCA, IncrementalPCA, TruncatedSVD, FastICA, NMF, KernelPCA, FactorAnalysis}
   - ferrotorch_ml::datasets::{make_classification, make_regression, make_blobs, make_moons, make_circles, load_iris, load_digits} (from ferrolearn-datasets)
5. For each re-exported estimator, the public API should accept &Tensor<T> on Cpu and return either a Tensor<T> result or an estimator object that can fit/transform/predict tensors.
6. Add a 'features' table in lib.rs docstring listing what's covered and what's deliberately not (e.g., 'not included: ferrolearn-linear etc., use ferrolearn directly').

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline before claiming done):
- This crate INHERENTLY moves tensors to CPU because ferrolearn is CPU-only. Make this VISIBLE: every function takes Tensor<T> on Device::Cpu and returns DeviceUnavailable on Cuda/Xpu/Meta with a message naming sklearn's CPU-only nature.
- Forbidden pattern: silently calling t.to(Device::Cpu) inside the adapter fn. The user must do it explicitly.
- Document in lib.rs: 'This crate is a sklearn-compat adapter. It is CPU-only by design. GPU tensors must be moved to CPU before calling these functions.'
- The follow-up question — should ferrolearn switch to ferray as its array backend? — is NOT this crate's call. Track separately.

Estimated effort: medium. Adapter is small; re-export surface is wide; testing matters because the round-trip must be lossless for f32/f64.

Closes audit gaps:
- docs/audit/05-ferrotorch-train.md gap on metrics (sklearn metrics suite)
- docs/audit/16-ferray-ferrolearn-integration.md Phase 3 in full
- Adds preprocessing, CV machinery, decomposition that have no current home in ferrotorch.
